inline_comments.message:
listElement will be a DataNode, so I am wondering why do we have two separate methods with same implementation.? 
If the intention is to make the explicit, then we should call deleteDataNode from the deleteListElement method.

I personally prefer 'onlySupportListNodeDeletion' as I feel it is clearer in terms of the overloaded method what the parameter actually is, and that only list node deletion is supported within this path.
Should we mention list here... the underlying collection may change e.g. from list to set and this could be missed... not sure perhaps it is ok as we are using list elements? What do others think?
Should this be inside the if statement?
This should be more descriptive, perhaps deletedAllListElements
nb - I dont think this name is describing what the method is doing. maybe rename the method or split it into two separate methods
not sure if the word 'data' is needed anymore and it is inconsistent with the delete method(s)
update teh wording to be in line as well!

tricky to split but maybe could be renamed to
deleteDataNodeOrDeleteCompleteListOfDataNodes
delete (to serach and ensure term 'delete' is used consistently for all such methods)
ok there are some doubts and misunderstandings about this boolean so I am suggesting this improved name : deletedAtLeastOneListElement
looks like it should be plural!

legacy issue: error scenarios should use wording like 'attempt to'
e.g. 'attempt is made to add list elements'
legacy: please rephrase, I dont understand what is being tested here
legacy: try to rephrase this scenario description is confusing
the idea is it replaces the whole contant (any old element(s)) with a given set of new elements

deleteListOrListElement might be clearer or even
deleteCompleteListOrListElement

an element cannot be empty.
Is this method only used for collections of list elements (if so rename accordingly!)

Mabe a more generic message is better (future proof) "No data nodes provided"

please rephrase

I think 'data fragment' should not be mention in the high level description of any of these tests (its a reference to what happens under the hood)
Should be || expectedException I think, but also could be removed as is always DataNodeNotFoundException
Yes, as you can add multiple list elements as part of one request.
I think this should remain as list, as the purpose of this api is to add elements to a list as it's defined in the yang module.

I think we should keep the name deleteDataNode for now as this is what the overridden method is also called, which does also delete a list element. We had a seperate method for deleting a list element, but this was removed in the last patchset as per Renu's comment.

What does the team think should this method name be changed to something like 
deleteDataNodeOrListData().
Yes, we remove the list elements within the if statement, so if they get removed deletedSomething = true.
We felt the test was too similar to deleting a list element with a key, as the behavior should not be different in the case of a grand child key.

create Jira to revisit/clean up these test
